steps.title: Translate minimum and maximum to inequalities
steps.content: The minimum recorded height is 42 feet. This means no tree is shorter than 42 feet, so all heights satisfy $t \geq 42$. The maximum recorded height is 57 feet. This means no tree is taller than 57 feet, so all heights satisfy $t \leq 57$.

steps.title: Combine constraints into a compound inequality
steps.content: Since both conditions must hold for all recorded heights in the area, we need both $t \geq 42$ AND $t \leq 57$. This combines into: $$42 \leq t \leq 57$$ This compound inequality bounds the height $t$ to the range from 42 to 57 feet, inclusive. That matches choice D — D is correct.
